coverage.note: No coverlet pass this version; V1 change is a single-file mutation that the new fixture catches (verified).

findings.issue: PLang test /BuilderSanity/BuilderSanity.test.goal FAILS with 'math.add: FormatException: The input string '[1, 2, 3]' was not in a correct format.' Coder v3 reported PLang 233/233; actual is 232/233.
findings.impact: Coder shipped with inaccurate test counts. The fixture relies on `set %items% = '[1, 2, 3]'` being auto-parsed into a list by `foreach`. Under current PLang semantics strings are atomic (data/this.cs:341 IsPlangIterable rejects string), so foreach yields the whole string once and the body's math.add chokes on the literal. Either a fixture bug (should construct a real list) or a deliberate semantics change that was never reflected in this smoke test. Either way the test is red and was claimed green.
findings.suggestion: Coder: update the fixture to construct a list (e.g. via list.create or `set %items% = [1, 2, 3]` if the builder supports it) OR delete the fixture if string→list auto-parse is intentionally gone. Also: every coder version on this branch (v1/v2/v3) is missing baseline-tests.md, so 'pre-existing' vs 'regression' can't be settled from the bot record — please write the baseline file on the next coder pass.

findings.issue: The new V1 fixture (StoreView_PropagatesIntoInnerDataProperties_NotHardcodedToOut) pins the View threading into json.Writer.EndRecord — the second M2 site. The first M2 site (Wire.cs:400-416, outer Wire.Write's own Properties walk) reads `View` directly with no intermediate writer, so structurally a similar regression there would be caught only if a Store-view test deposited a [Sensitive]-bearing value into the outer Data's Properties (not the inner's). No such test exists today. Risk is low because the outer-Properties path uses View as a local variable — there's no field to silently revert to a default — but a symmetric fixture would prevent future drift if someone refactors Wire.Write to construct the json.Writer earlier and accidentally drops the View arg.
findings.impact: Not a current bug — coverage gap that would have to coincide with a refactor to materialize. Low priority.
findings.suggestion: Optional: add a sibling fixture that puts the [Sensitive]-carrying List<Identity> into outer.Properties (not inner.Properties) and asserts the same Out-excludes / Store-includes contract on the outer's Properties block.

findings.issue: json.Writer's constructor has a third arg defaulted to View.Out. Wire.Write passes View explicitly (line 392), so the default path is never exercised in production. Tests don't pin the default either — if a future caller constructs json.Writer directly and forgets the third arg, it'll silently get Out-mode filtering on Store/Debug-intended writes.
findings.impact: Defensive — would only matter if a new Writer call site appears.
findings.suggestion: Optional: a tiny unit test instantiating `new app.channels.serializers.json.Writer(jsonWriter)` and asserting EndRecord uses View.Out, or just remove the default and force every caller to be explicit.

summary: C# suite green (3381/3381). PLang suite red (232/233): one failing test — /BuilderSanity/BuilderSanity.test.goal — that coder v3 reported as passing (claimed 233/233). The new V1 fixture for the json.Writer.EndRecord view-threading fix is honest and mutation-verified (reverting `_view` to `View.Out` in EndRecord makes the Store assertion fail; restored). Two minor coverage notes on adjacent Wire/Writer paths. Process violation: no baseline-tests.md in any coder version on this branch.
